name: Planning default and inline-plan clarification
description: Clarify the harness and project-owned workflow skills so non-trivial tasks always show a plan artifact before execution, distinguish tiny one-shot exceptions from normal Level 1 multi-step work, and make verification-before-completion explicit before done claims.

evidence.description: Session rules now require the orchestrator to state the planning mode before execution and to apply verification-before-completion discipline explicitly before any done/ready claim.
Plan router now distinguishes tiny direct-task notes, explicit inline plans, and full ExecPlans so small multi-step work is no longer implicitly plan-less.
Project-owned skill overview now states that every task has a planning mode and that completion claims require an explicit verification-before-completion pass.
Entrypoint triage rules now require a visible planning mode for every task and explicitly push multi-step small work into visible inline planning.
Ceremony guidance now narrows Level 0 to truly one-shot mechanical work and requires explicit inline plans for normal Level 1 multi-step tasks.
Plan-writing guidance now states clearly that full ExecPlans are only one planning mode and that smaller tasks still require direct-task notes or inline plans.
Verification guidance now requires the final verification pass to be explicit in the handoff rather than left implicit.
Full repository verification passed after the workflow-doc and harness updates.
Final GitNexus change detection reported LOW risk with no affected execution flows.
